name: Xandr Programmatic Advertising Platform API
what_it_does: Programmatic advertising platform (DSP + SSP) with REST API — formerly AppNexus, acquired by AT&T (2018) and Microsoft (2021), providing demand-side platform for advertisers, supply-side platform for publishers, data marketplace, and curated deals for premium programmatic inventory across display, video, and native.
use_cases: Managing programmatic ad campaigns across display, video, and native via Xandr DSP API
Accessing curated private marketplace (PMP) deals for premium inventory programmatically
Pulling campaign performance data and attribution metrics for marketing analytics
Managing audience segments and data marketplace buys via Xandr API
Integrating Xandr with Microsoft Advertising for cross-channel media planning
not_for: Social advertising on Meta/TikTok (Xandr accesses open internet inventory, not social walled gardens)
Small advertisers — Xandr is enterprise programmatic with significant minimum requirements
Search advertising (use Microsoft Advertising API for Bing search)
best_when: An enterprise advertiser or agency needs access to premium programmatic inventory through curated deals, private marketplaces, and Microsoft's media properties — especially for cross-channel campaigns including Microsoft Advertising and Xandr.
avoid_when: You don't have programmatic advertising expertise in-house, have spend below $50K/month, or primarily need social media advertising platforms.

auth.notes: Username/password authentication generating session token. Session token in Authorization header. Member-scoped access (advertiser member ID required). No OAuth2. No webhooks — must poll for reporting. Async reporting model. Microsoft Advertising API separate for search. Curated deals via Deal API. Publisher SSP access for supply-side management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; enterprise access required
pricing.paid_tiers: Technology fee as percentage of spend; contact Microsoft/Xandr sales
pricing.notes: Originally AppNexus. Acquired by AT&T/WarnerMedia 2018 for $1.6B, renamed Xandr. Acquired by Microsoft 2021 for ~$1B. Now integrated into Microsoft Advertising ecosystem. Strong in premium publisher direct deals and connected TV. Competing with Google DV360 and The Trade Desk. Microsoft's ad tech stack integration adds scale. Publisher-side SSP business gives insight into inventory quality.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per member account; enterprise limits available

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for campaigns, line items, creatives, audiences, deals, and reporting. Session token auth via username/password. Good developer documentation (AppNexus legacy docs extensive). Async reporting model — generate, poll, download. No webhooks. Member ID required for all API calls. Microsoft integration adds complexity to platform strategy.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Campaign reads fully idempotent. Deal reads idempotent. Report generation not idempotent — use report ID for polling. Creative uploads not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Session token auth (username/password) — no OAuth2; token expires and requires re-authentication
Async reporting — generate report request, poll status, download when ready
No webhooks — polling required for all status updates
Microsoft acquisition creates integration complexity — some features migrating to Microsoft Advertising
Member ID required in all API calls — obtain during account setup

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, CCPA compliant. US and EU data residency. Session token auth (username/password based) is weaker than OAuth2 — rotate credentials regularly. Microsoft ownership brings enterprise security standards. Audience data and bidding data may include sensitive behavioral segments — implement data governance. Member credentials should be service account, not personal credentials.
